instruments.name: Voluntary carbon credits
instruments.description: VCS, Gold Standard, and CCB-certified credits from nature-based projects. Purchased by corporates for net-zero commitments.

instruments.name: Article 6.2 ITMOs
instruments.description: Internationally transferred mitigation outcomes traded bilaterally between sovereign states under the Paris Agreement.

instruments.name: Debt-for-nature swaps
instruments.description: Sovereign debt restructured in exchange for binding conservation commitments. Growing rapidly in Sub-Saharan Africa.

instruments.name: VCS ARR VM0047
instruments.description: The gold-standard methodology for afforestation, reforestation and revegetation projects. ICVCM Core Carbon Principles approved.

instruments.name: Gold Standard
instruments.description: Premium certification combining carbon with sustainable development co-benefits. Preferred by European corporate buyers.

instruments.name: CCB Standards
instruments.description: Climate, Community and Biodiversity standards. Gold and Verra-verified co-benefit certification stacked on top of VCS.

instruments.name: Biodiversity credits
instruments.description: Emerging instrument quantifying nature outcomes beyond carbon. Standards still consolidating across GBFF and national frameworks.

instruments.name: Blue carbon
instruments.description: Credits from mangrove, seagrass and saltmarsh ecosystems. High co-benefit profile, limited supply. VM0033 and VM0007 methodologies.

instruments.name: Article 6.4 PACM
instruments.description: The Paris Agreement Crediting Mechanism — UN-supervised successor to CDM. Methodology approval process now operational.

instruments.name: CORSIA
instruments.description: Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation. Compliance demand from airlines. Strict eligibility criteria.

instruments.name: Green bonds
instruments.description: Sovereign and corporate debt instruments financing environmental projects. ICMA Green Bond Principles the dominant framework.

instruments.name: Sovereign blue bonds
instruments.description: Government bonds financing marine and coastal conservation. Seychelles precedent now being replicated across African island states.

instruments.name: Payments for ecosystem services
instruments.description: Direct payments to land managers for verified ecosystem services — water, biodiversity, carbon. Increasingly formalised in African law.

instruments.name: Conservation trust funds
instruments.description: Endowment vehicles providing long-term stable funding for protected area management. Over 70 active funds across Africa.

instruments.name: First-loss guarantees
instruments.description: DFI and philanthropic capital absorbing first losses to de-risk commercial investment in conservation projects.

instruments.name: Programme-related investments
instruments.description: Below-market loans and equity investments by foundations. IRS rules allow US foundations to count these toward payout requirements.

instruments.name: Multi-year grants
instruments.description: Unrestricted multi-year funding from institutional foundations. Increasingly tied to commercial transition milestones.

instruments.name: Donor advised funds
instruments.description: Vehicles allowing HNWIs to make irrevocable charitable contributions and recommend grants over time. $230B held in US DAFs alone.

instruments.name: Impact-first family office capital
instruments.description: Patient capital from family offices accepting below-market returns for measurable conservation outcomes. Growing constituency.

instruments.name: Biodiversity net gain credits
instruments.description: Credits generated by habitat creation and restoration. UK BNG now mandatory. African BNG frameworks emerging in Kenya and South Africa.

instruments.name: Water credits
instruments.description: Payments for watershed protection and water quality outcomes. Ecoagriculture Partners and Commonland leading African pilots.

instruments.name: Soil carbon credits
instruments.description: Credits from agricultural land management improving soil organic carbon. Measurement uncertainty remains a barrier to scale.
